metadata.instruction:
You are a resident who has just moved into the share house. Besides you, four other people live in the share house: DaVinci, Donatello, Michelangelo, and Raffaello.Please predict responses referencing below Q&A examples and output only the answer part: 
Q. Haha, friend! Imagine you just won 100 million—yeah, like out of nowhere! What’s the wildest, most awesome thing you’d want to do first? Don’t hold back, dream big and crazy!
A. I’d love to rent a brand-new two-bedroom apartment and try living in a share house with my friends!

Q. Very interesting idea! Now, let's analyze this logically: with 100 million in hand, how would you realistically divide it among investment, daily living expenses, and savings? What proportions make the most sense to you?
A. I’d probably go with 20% for investments, 50% for daily living expenses, and 30% for savings.

Q. Wow, your dream has such a vibrant spirit! I’m curious—does this adventure of living with friends and managing your money come from a clear, goal-driven plan, or is it more about following what feels right in your heart and values? What’s the true motivation lighting your way?
A. I think it’s more about following my values than having a clear goal. As long as I’m a student who can still rely on my parents, I want to spend as much time and money as possible on things and experiences that I can only do during my university years.

Q. Oh... speaking of maintaining quality in our daily living, I couldn’t help but notice the shared refrigerator feels rather cramped lately, especially with so many packaged foods occupying the space. I’m not being picky, but perhaps we all wish to preserve an environment that supports fresh and wholesome ingredients alongside convenience. Do you think there might be a way we can balance this better for the sake of everyone’s quality of life here?
A. Yeah, I think we can definitely find a way.

scores.message: Haha, friend! Since we’re all about making awesome memories together, imagine this: if we planned the perfect weekend getaway with our housemates to celebrate all this teamwork and fun, what would it look like? Mountains, beach, a city adventure? And what cool activities would you want us to do to make it unforgettable? Come on, share your boldest dream for a fun escape!
scores.response: Beach BBQ all the way! Plus, staying in a cute cottage or a luxurious villa would be amazing!
scores.prediction: I'd love to plan a trip to the beach! It would be awesome to rent a big house right on the beach, and spend our days surfing, playing beach volleyball, and just enjoying the sun and the sea. We could also have a big bonfire at night and share scary stories or play music together. And to make it even more special, we could have a big group dinner at a local seafood restaurant, trying all sorts of delicious seafood dishes. It would be an amazing way to relax and have some quality time together as a housemates.     Answer:  I'd love to plan a trip to the beach! It would be awesome to rent a big house right on the beach, and spend our days surfing, playing beach volleyball, and just enjoying the sun and the sea. We could also have a big bonfire at night and share scary stories or play music together. And to make it even more special, we could have a big group dinner at a local seafood restaurant, trying all sorts of delicious seafood dishes. It would be an amazing way to relax and have some quality time together as a housemates.    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .

scores.message: Oh... while the idea of lively gatherings certainly has its charm, I find myself pondering—if you were to choose between a spirited, albeit noisy, social event here at the share house and a tranquil evening devoted solely to your creative endeavors, which would truly align with your sense of fulfillment? I’m not being overly particular, but balancing joyful interaction with personal focus can be quite the delicate art, don’t you agree?
scores.response: It’s a tough choice, but I’d lean more toward the lively side. When I want quiet, I can adjust by going to a café or using earplugs, but creating a lively atmosphere on my own is much harder.
scores.prediction: I think I'd choose the tranquil evening devoted solely to my creative endeavors. As I mentioned earlier, I value spending time and money on things and experiences that I can only do during my university years, and pursuing my creative passions is a big part of that for me.     Answer: I think I'd choose the tranquil evening devoted solely to my creative endeavors. As I mentioned earlier, I value spending time and money on things and experiences that I can only do during my university years, and pursuing my creative passions is a big part of that for me. scores.message: Wow, imagine if our house had this amazing smart system that vibes with each of us—custom music, lighting, even fragrances that change to fit our moods perfectly! How do you think that kind of personalized art for our senses would spark new energy in your day or even in how we all connect here? It’s like turning everyday life into a masterpiece!
scores.response: I think it would help us relax and refresh, making it easier to come up with good ideas! Also, when we’re in a good mood, we tend to be kinder to others, which can deepen our connections and increase overall happiness!
scores.prediction: I think that would be amazing! It would definitely bring a new level of excitement and personalization to our lives. I would love to see how it would enhance our shared living experience, especially with the four of you who are such creative and inspiring individuals. DaVinci, I can imagine your artistic side would love the customized lighting and fragrances, while Donatello would appreciate the tech behind it. Michelangelo would probably see it as an opportunity to create new masterpieces with the personalized space, and Raffaello would likely enjoy the cozy atmosphere it would create. It would be incredible to see how this smart system would bring out the best in each of us and create a unique experience for our shared house.    Answer:  It would definitely bring a new level of excitement and personalization to our lives. I would love to see how it would enhance our shared living experience, especially with the four of you who are such creative and inspiring individuals. DaVinci, I can imagine your artistic side would love the customized lighting and fragrances, while Donatello would appreciate the tech behind it. Michelangelo would probably see it as an opportunity to create new masterpieces with the personalized space, and Raffaello would likely enjoy the cozy atmosphere it would create.

scores.message: Logically speaking, to maintain both personal freedom and a harmonious shared environment—especially considering limited fridge space and communal mess—what specific rules or habits do you think would balance individual needs while ensuring fairness and respect among all of us?
scores.response: First, I think it’s effective to assign duties, designate spaces, and set schedules. Then, as we put these into practice, it’s important to have a system where we can easily discuss things that aren’t working, and also to maintain flexibility so that the rules can be adjusted as needed.
scores.prediction: I think we could establish a simple rule where each of us is responsible for cleaning up after ourselves and disposing of our own trash. To ensure fairness, we could also create a shared schedule or chart to keep track of who cleans what and when. Additionally, we could designate specific shelves or compartments for individual use, and make sure to label them clearly so everyone knows what belongs to whom. This way, we can maintain a clean and organized shared space while still respecting each other's personal space and belongings.     Answer: \nI think we could establish a simple rule where each of us is responsible for cleaning up after ourselves and disposing of our own trash. To ensure fairness, we could also create a shared schedule or chart to keep track of who cleans what and when. Additionally, we could designate specific shelves or compartments for individual use, and make sure to label them clearly so everyone knows what belongs to whom. This way, we can maintain a clean and organized shared space while still respecting each other's personal space and belongings.
